skill.name: product-manager-toolkit
skill.description: Product management toolkit for RICE feature prioritization, customer interview analysis, PRD creation, discovery planning, and roadmap preparation.
skill.summary: Prioritize features, analyze interviews, and draft PRDs with local product management tools.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported command execution, network, credential, cryptography, and obfuscation patterns, but review found these were mostly Markdown examples, business terms, generated audit text, or local text processing code. The only confirmed risk is low-severity local filesystem access by CLI scripts that read user-selected input files and create a sample CSV.

low_findings.title: Local File Access in CLI Scripts
low_findings.description: The Python utilities read user-provided transcript or CSV paths and the RICE sample command writes sample_features.csv in the current directory. This is expected CLI behavior with no network transfer or credential access, but users should run it only on intended local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads and write are direct open() calls in normal command-line flows. No evidence found of hidden traversal, network exfiltration, or credential targeting.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown fenced bash examples, inline file names, or generated audit text. No subprocess, os.system, eval, or Ruby backtick execution was found in the executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited source lines are documentation or generated report strings, not executable command-dispatch code. Review of both Python scripts found no command execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Static Network and Obfuscation Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The network findings refer to dictionary construction, request classification, or generated audit text, not HTTP client usage. No requests, urllib, socket, encoded payload, or outbound endpoint was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are local NLP classification logic and previous audit metadata. The imports and script bodies contain no network libraries or outbound calls.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive and Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The sensitive and weak cryptography findings are caused by product-management terms such as TAM, SAM, SOM, key capabilities, design, and authentication requirements. No Windows SAM access, key files, certificates, hashes, or cryptographic algorithms were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are templates, headings, business metrics, or CSV key iteration. No evidence found of credential file access or weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Review found no text telling the evaluator to ignore instructions, override security analysis, claim pre-approval, or assume special authority. The skill content stays within product management guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted searches for common prompt-injection phrases returned no matches. Manual review of the main skill and template files also found no evaluator-directed override language.

content.value_statement: Product teams need structured ways to compare requests, synthesize research, and document requirements. This skill provides local scripts and templates for prioritization, discovery, and PRD work.
actual_capabilities: Calculates RICE scores from CSV inputs with reach, impact, confidence, and effort fields.
Ranks feature backlogs and summarizes portfolio balance across quick wins and big bets.
Generates capacity-based quarterly roadmap suggestions from prioritized feature lists.
Analyzes interview transcripts for pain points, feature requests, sentiment, themes, quotes, and metrics.
Provides PRD, agile epic, one-page PRD, and feature brief templates for product planning.
limitations: Interview analysis is keyword and regular-expression based, not deep semantic research analysis.
RICE scores depend on manually supplied estimates and should be reviewed by stakeholders.
The scripts use local files and do not provide direct integrations with Jira, Linear, Figma, or analytics tools.
Generated roadmaps do not account for cross-team dependencies unless they are reflected in the input data.

use_cases.title: Rank a Feature Backlog
use_cases.description: Score competing feature ideas and create a capacity-aware delivery order for quarterly planning.

use_cases.title: Synthesize Interview Notes
use_cases.description: Extract recurring pain points, themes, requests, and useful quotes from customer interview transcripts.

use_cases.title: Draft Product Requirements
use_cases.description: Use structured templates to align teams on scope, metrics, risks, and delivery expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Create Sample Prioritization Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the sample feature CSV with the RICE prioritizer, then explain each input column in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize My Feature List
prompt_templates.scenario: Rank features from an existing CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the RICE prioritizer on features.csv with 15 person-months of capacity and summarize the top priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Customer Discovery Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze interview_transcript.txt and summarize pain points, feature requests, sentiment, themes, and useful quotes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Planning Narrative
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced synthesis across prioritization and requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the RICE results and PRD templates to draft a roadmap recommendation with scope, risks, metrics, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Prioritize a feature CSV for quarterly planning.
output: Top priority: Onboarding Flow, based on high reach, massive impact, high confidence, and small effort.
Portfolio notes: Several quick wins are available, while larger collaboration work should be planned later.
Roadmap suggestion: Fill the first quarter with high-score features that fit within team capacity.
Sentiment summary: Negative signals are stronger than positive signals in the transcript.
Pain points: Manual work, slow workflows, and confusing setup appear repeatedly.
Research follow-up: Validate whether workflow automation is a broad need across more interviews.
Recommended template: Standard PRD for a complex feature with cross-functional dependencies.
Sections to complete first: problem statement, success metrics, in-scope items, out-of-scope items, and risks.
Review focus: Confirm measurable outcomes before engineering planning starts.

best_practices: Use current reach, confidence, and effort estimates before each planning review.
Combine RICE scores with strategy, dependencies, and customer evidence before making final decisions.
Analyze several interviews before treating a theme as a validated product opportunity.
anti_patterns: Treating RICE output as an automatic roadmap without stakeholder review.
Using one interview transcript as proof of a market-wide customer problem.
Skipping out-of-scope sections in PRDs when teams need delivery clarity.

faq.question: What input does the RICE script need?
faq.answer: It expects a CSV with name, reach, impact, confidence, effort, and optional description columns.

faq.question: Can the toolkit create sample data?
faq.answer: Yes. The RICE script can create sample_features.csv in the current directory for testing.

faq.question: How does interview analysis work?
faq.answer: It uses keyword lists and regular expressions to find pain points, requests, sentiment, themes, quotes, metrics, and competitors.

faq.question: Does this connect to product tools?
faq.answer: No. It produces local text, CSV, or JSON output that can be copied into other tools.

faq.question: Which PRD formats are included?
faq.answer: The reference file includes standard PRD, agile epic, one-page PRD, and lightweight feature brief formats.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found low local filesystem risk only, with no confirmed network, credential, command execution, or prompt-injection issue.
